name: 14-day Sweet Pickles
recipeInstructions:
(If packed whole, use cucumbers of uniform size)
Can be canned whole, in strips, or in slices.
Yield: About 5 to 9 pints
Procedure:
Wash cucumbers. Cut a 1/16-inch slice off the blossom end and discard, but leave 1/4-inch of stem attached. Place whole cucumbers in a suitable 1-gallon container. Add 1/4 cup canning or pickling salt to 2 quarts water and bring to a boil. Pour over cucumbers. Add a suitable cover and weight. Place a clean towel over the container and keep the temperature at about 70 degrees F.
On the third and fifth days, drain the salt water and discard. Rinse cucumbers and rescald the cover and weight. Return cucumbers to the container. Add 1/4 cup salt to 2 quarts fresh water and boil. Pour over cucumbers. Replace the cover and weight, and re-cover with a clean towel.
On the seventh day, drain the salt water and discard. Rinse cucumbers and rescald the containers, cover, and weight. Slice or strip cucumbers, if desired, and return to the container. Place celery seed and pickling spices in a small cheesecloth bag. Combine 2 cups sugar and 4 cups vinegar in a saucepan. Add the spice bag, bring to a boil, and pour the pickling solution over cucumbers. Add the cover and weight, and re-cover with a clean towel.
On each of the next six days, drain the syrup and spice bag and save. Add 1/2 cup sugar each day and bring to a boil in a saucepan. Remove the cucumbers and rinse. Scald the container, cover, and weight daily. Return the cucumbers to the container, add boiled syrup, cover, weight, and re-cover with towel.
On the 14th day, drain the syrup into a saucepan. Fill sterile pint jars (for more information see "Jars and Lids") or clean quart jars, leaving 1/2-inch headspace. Add 1/2 cup sugar to syrup and bring to boil. Remove the spice bag. Pour hot syrup over cucumbers, leaving 1/2-inch headspace. Adjust lids and process according to the recommendations in Table 1 or use a low-temperature pasteurization treatment. (For more information see "Low-Temperature Pasteurization Treatment").
